title: halenia major subsp. meridensis
intro: Halenia major subsp. meridensis, also known as Andean halenia, is a plant species within the Gentianaceae family, native to the Andes mountain range in South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antimicrobial agent to combat infections. Halenia major subsp. meridensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
